jokes.text: I tell you, I've been looking forward to this for... 29 days!
jokes.setup: Monthly dinner celebration
jokes.punchline: Overly specific count of 29 days shows obsessive tracking
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Mac's enthusiasm and over-precision about mundane things

jokes.text: I did my hair good, and I wore two colognes.
jokes.setup: Mac's preparation for their monthly dinner
jokes.punchline: Two colognes as if that's a good idea
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac character moment - his misguided attempts at sophistication

jokes.text: Maybe we'll get lucky and watch one of these dumb-ass waiters spill spaghetti sauce all over himself. And fall down or something.
jokes.setup: Being seated near the kitchen
jokes.punchline: Dennis hoping for others' misfortune as entertainment
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis sociopathy disguised as casual conversation

jokes.text: Or the anniversary of us moving in together, which is like an anniver-- it's been a long time. Anniversary is what it is, really.
jokes.setup: Mac trying to explain their relationship to the hostess
jokes.punchline: Awkward verbal stumbling over what to call their relationship
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment as Mac overexplains their mundane relationship

jokes.text: I was trying to feel your breast.
jokes.setup: Mac reaching for something from the hostess
jokes.punchline: Blunt admission of inappropriate touching
jokes.explanation: Perfectly delivered shock line - Mac's oblivious honesty about his creepy behavior

jokes.text: I'm sure at some point they'll come over to pay tribute.
jokes.setup: Mac and Dennis at another table
jokes.punchline: Frank expecting 'tribute' like he's royalty
jokes.explanation: Great Frank delusion - treating a casual dinner like a royal court

jokes.text: They're calling me 'Scarecrow.' 'Cause I'm all alone in the cornfields, scaring away the other birds.
jokes.setup: Dee imagining what others think of her dining alone
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and self-deprecating bird metaphor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dee moment - her bird-related insecurities manifest in paranoid self-talk

jokes.text: I have a Groupon for this evening that expires tonight.
jokes.setup: Dee insisting she doesn't need to leave
jokes.punchline: Reveals the pathetic real reason she can't leave
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Dee's fake confidence - she's trapped by a coupon

jokes.text: Can't believe this. They still haven't paid tribute. It's been ten minutes.
jokes.setup: Frank still waiting for acknowledgment
jokes.punchline: Escalating indignation over lack of 'tribute'
jokes.explanation: Good callback escalation of Frank's delusional entitlement

jokes.text: My eyes, they slant down. I don't have a good peripheral.
jokes.setup: Dennis asking Mac to look subtly at the other table
jokes.punchline: Mac's weirdly technical explanation of his visual limitations
jokes.explanation: Mac's bizarre anatomical excuse for not being able to spy effectively

jokes.text: I'm already sitting in the wobbliest chair in the entire restaurant.
jokes.setup: Dennis complaining about their table situation
jokes.punchline: Hyperbolic victimization over a minor furniture issue
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis melodrama. The superlative 'wobbliest chair in the entire restaurant' shows his dramatic self-pity.

jokes.text: I don't want to sit on a pile of matches.
jokes.setup: Waiter offering matches to fix wobbly chair
jokes.punchline: Dennis's prissy rejection of practical solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis vanity - too good for a makeshift solution

jokes.text: Here I am, a regular, a full-paying customer, and I'm sat by the kitchen on the wobbliest chair in Philadelphia. You know, forced to eat dinner with this swarm of coupon-waving trash.
jokes.setup: Dennis complaining about treatment
jokes.punchline: 'Swarm of coupon-waving trash' - extreme classism
jokes.explanation: Peak Dennis elitism - 'swarm of coupon-waving trash' is perfectly awful

jokes.text: There's an old, short, fat man here. He's sat with his young sleeping partner. Bring them a glass of the house red from us.
jokes.setup: Dennis sending wine to Frank and Charlie
jokes.punchline: Cruel description of Frank while pretending to be generous
jokes.explanation: Dennis's passive-aggressive 'generosity' with vicious description

jokes.text: Living with you has... changed my life. I-I used to be unaware...
jokes.setup: Frank starting a heartfelt speech to Charlie
jokes.punchline: Interrupted mid-sentence by waiter, killing the moment
jokes.explanation: Good timing comedy - Frank's rare vulnerability immediately interrupted

jokes.text: One glass of wine for two people, and house red? That wasn't nice. They're just trying to agitate us.
jokes.setup: Receiving wine from Dennis and Mac
jokes.punchline: Frank interpreting generosity as an attack
jokes.explanation: Frank's paranoid interpretation of what was actually a slight

jokes.text: There are men here, and they're watching me.
jokes.setup: Dee grabbing a waiter for help
jokes.punchline: Paranoid delusion about being watched
jokes.explanation: Dee's social anxiety manifesting as conspiracy thinking

jokes.text: Is that table having a meal consisting only of loud noise, screams, and hollers?
jokes.setup: Frank and Charlie being loud
jokes.punchline: Dennis's prissy description of their celebration
jokes.explanation: Perfectly snooty Dennis observation - describing celebration as dining on noise

jokes.text: Well, maybe my old pal, uh, Abraham Lincoln begs to differ.
jokes.setup: Dennis pulling out money about chair issue
jokes.punchline: Pretentious reference to the $5 bill
jokes.explanation: Peak Dennis pretension - addressing money as historical figures

jokes.text: Maybe this George Washington will make it come here faster.
jokes.setup: Following up the Lincoln reference
jokes.punchline: Doubling down on the money-as-presidents bit
jokes.explanation: Good callback that escalates Dennis's pretentious money bit

jokes.text: We'll say, we don't want that. You know? We'll-we'll tell them that, uh, what is it, Chilean wine is out of season, and that their taste in wine in general is very poor.
jokes.setup: Frank planning response to wine gift
jokes.punchline: 'Chilean wine is out of season' - nonsensical wine criticism
jokes.explanation: Great Frank absurdity - 'wine being out of season' shows his fake sophistication

jokes.text: I'm just gonna pour the wine out.
jokes.setup: Dennis getting frustrated with the wine situation
jokes.punchline: Petty dramatic gesture of wasting wine
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Dennis's childish response to perceived slight

jokes.text: Those two guys don't give a shit about you, Charlie. You bought a business together; they made you a janitor.
jokes.setup: Frank and Charlie discussing Mac and Dennis
jokes.punchline: Harsh truth about Charlie's exploitation
jokes.explanation: Brutal truth delivered casually - exposes the gang's treatment of Charlie

jokes.text: Tie his shoelaces together. You want me to... tie a customer's shoelaces together?
jokes.setup: Dee asking waiter to prank Dennis
jokes.punchline: Waiter's disbelief at the ridiculous request
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Dee's childish plan and waiter's reasonable confusion

jokes.text: 'Oh, no, I-I would lose my job. And I wouldn't be able to work at Gugino's anymore, and my family, and they'd be so disappointed 'cause they're currently so proud of my accomplishments.' I mean, God, lighten up, guy.
jokes.setup: Waiter refusing to tie customer's shoelaces
jokes.punchline: Dee mocks waiter's job pride while asking him to sabotage customers
jokes.explanation: Classic Dee cruelty disguised as joking. The sarcastic family disappointment bit is particularly vicious.

jokes.text: Oh, no, I-I would lose my job. And I wouldn't be able to work at Gugino's anymore, and my family, and they'd be so disappointed 'cause they're currently so proud of my accomplishments.
jokes.setup: Waiter worried about his job
jokes.punchline: Dee's sarcastic mockery of working at a restaurant
jokes.explanation: Classic Dee cruelty - mocking someone's livelihood with fake sympathy

jokes.text: When I wanted to get the karaoke machine for our apartment, you laughed in my face.
jokes.setup: Mac revealing his real issue with Dennis
jokes.punchline: Petty grievance about a karaoke machine
jokes.explanation: Good Mac pathetic moment - huge emotional investment in trivial rejection

jokes.text: Your hair looks small.
jokes.setup: Mac demanding Dennis say something nice
jokes.punchline: Dennis's idea of a compliment is bizarre and insulting
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd insult disguised as compliment - perfectly captures Dennis's inability to be genuinely nice

jokes.text: You remember that night at Dooley's pool party? On that fine summer eve, when I did that double jackknife twist and blew everybody's tits off?
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to compliment Mac
jokes.punchline: Story that's entirely about Dennis's own achievements
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis narcissism - 'complimenting' Mac with story about his own diving prowess

jokes.text: And then I went down on Chrissie Orlando on the trampoline later that night?
jokes.setup: Dennis continuing his 'compliment' story
jokes.punchline: Adding graphic sexual detail to public speech
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Dennis's inappropriate oversharing

jokes.text: Frank Reynolds, Charlie Kelly. We would like to make a toast.
jokes.setup: Frank and Charlie addressing entire restaurant
jokes.punchline: Taking their petty restaurant drama public
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - making private grievances into public spectacle

jokes.text: Those people refuse to drink. Yeah. They don't know how to pay tribute. They hate America! They hate the troops.
jokes.setup: Frank pointing out people without sambuca
jokes.punchline: Escalating to accusations of troop hatred
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from restaurant pettiness to patriotic accusation

jokes.text: They hate America! They hate the troops. They hate the people who work hardest for them. They turn them into janitors.
jokes.setup: Frank continuing his anti-Dennis and Mac speech
jokes.punchline: Escalates to calling them anti-American while referencing Charlie's janitor status
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation tying patriotism to the Charlie janitor issue. Uses nationalism to weaponize personal grievance.

jokes.text: He doesn't hide under a toupee. He faces his challenges, instead of just retreating to the sewers... nude to forage for rings and coins.
jokes.setup: Dennis making speech about the soldier
jokes.punchline: Bizarre specific dig at Charlie's lifestyle
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and absurd - Dennis's weirdly accurate description of Charlie

jokes.text: He doesn't hide under a toupee. He faces his challenges, instead of just retreating to the sewers... nude to forage for rings and coins.
jokes.setup: Dennis giving speech about the veteran
jokes.punchline: Describes Charlie's life in increasingly dehumanizing terms
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly vicious character assassination disguised as honoring troops. 'Nude to forage for rings and coins' paints Charlie as subhuman.

jokes.text: And I licked her asshole a little bit. It was pretty good. It was all right. It wasn't great. But it was fine.
jokes.setup: Dennis continuing his public sexual story
jokes.punchline: Graphic detail with casual restaurant review tone
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe - Dennis reviewing oral sex like food with complete lack of awareness

jokes.text: ♪ Did you ever know That you're my hero? 'Cause you are the wind Beneath my wings. ♪
jokes.setup: Dennis's increasingly inappropriate speech
jokes.punchline: Breaking into song in the middle of restaurant
jokes.explanation: Good escalation to peak embarrassment - Dennis singing to confused soldier

jokes.text: I was talking about you. That was about me? Yes. He wasn't there when I went down on Chrissy Orlando.
jokes.setup: Mac confused about Dennis's speech
jokes.punchline: Dennis revealing the soldier wasn't even at the party
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection reveal - Dennis's entire emotional speech was misdirected

jokes.text: Those goddamn sons of bitches. They turned up the A/C. Because they knew we were sitting right underneath a vent.
jokes.setup: Dennis feeling cold air
jokes.punchline: Paranoid theory about Frank and Charlie controlling temperature
jokes.explanation: Good paranoid escalation - Dennis attributing restaurant comfort to enemy action

jokes.text: I'll pinch their dicks with this lobster.
jokes.setup: Mac planning revenge
jokes.punchline: Using restaurant lobster as weapon in bizarre way
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd weapon choice - using lobster claw as torture device

jokes.text: Hey, his shoelaces are tied together.
jokes.setup: Dennis falling down
jokes.punchline: Revelation that Dee's earlier plan actually worked
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to earlier setup - Dee's ridiculous plan actually succeeded

jokes.text: I've wanted to see that all night long.
jokes.setup: Dennis seeing waiter fall with spaghetti
jokes.punchline: Callback to his earlier wish for exactly this
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to opening - Dennis gets exactly what he wanted

jokes.text: Have you been here this entire time?
jokes.setup: Gang discovering Dee orchestrated the prank
jokes.punchline: Frank's shocked realization Dee was there all along
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat - Frank realizing Dee orchestrated everything from the shadows
